zh.Coningsby, Lincolnshire, England, United: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾康寧斯比
zh.Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, England,: 英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾索普 阿諾德
zh.Fersfield, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可費斯費爾德
zh.Coningsby, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾康寧斯比
zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Bois I於1270年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾索普 阿諾德時，他的父親Edmund de Bois28歲，母親Joan de Bois15歲。他於大約1287年與Isolda Lapy在英國英格蘭諾福爾可費斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1311年11月16日在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾康寧斯比享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Bois I于1270年出生在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾索普 阿諾德时，他的父亲Edmund de Bois28岁，母亲Joan de Bois15岁。他于大约1287年与Isolda Lapy在英国英格兰諾福爾可費斯費爾德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1311年11月16日在英国英格兰林可爾史爾康寧斯比享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Bois I was born in 1270, in Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Edmund de Bois, was 28 and his mother, Joan de Bois, was 15. He married Isolda Lapy about 1287, in Fersfield, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 16 November 1311, in Coningsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Bois I est né en 1270, à Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edmund de Bois, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Joan de Bois, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Isolda Lapy environ 1287, à Fersfield, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 16 novembre 1311, à Coningsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Bois Iが1270年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアソルペ アルノルドで生まれた時，父Edmund de Boisは28歳，母Joan de Boisは15歳であった。約1287年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクフエルスフィールドでIsolda Lapyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1311年11月16日にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアコニングスビーで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Bois I(이)가 1270년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 소 아노드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund de Bois(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Bois(은)는 15세였다. 그는 대략 1287년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 퍼스필드에서 Isolda Lapy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1311년 11월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 코닝스비에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Bois I родился в 1270 г. в местности таурп арнолд, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Edmund de Bois было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Bois, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Isolda Lapy около 1287 г. в местности фирсфиелд, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 16 ноября 1311 г. в местности конингсби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 41 год.
